summary: Batch item decompilation PR across many item files. The PR body describes 24 item functions and a clean `ninja`, `ninja diff`, and objdiff test plan; the final diff slice visibly replaces 18 `/// #` placeholders with C and the decomp.dev report records +1996 matched bytes, +0.05%, 14 new 100% matches plus several improved items. The main reusable outcome is the review-driven move from overloading generic Pokemon item vars to adding a dedicated `itKabigon_ItemVars` struct and `kabigon` union member.
what_changed: Implemented item callbacks/logic routines in the final diff for `it_3F14_Logic7_Thrown`, `it_3F14_Logic7_DmgDealt`, `itGreatfoxlaser_UnkMotion1_Phys`, `it_802C9D40`, `itKabigon_UnkMotion0_Anim`, `it_802CA3F4`, `itLugia_UnkMotion4_Phys`, `it_802BE5D8`, `it_802BE958`, `itOldottosea_UnkMotion7_Coll`, `it_802E0D9C`, `it_802B43D0`, `itTincle_UnkMotion4_Phys`, `itTincle_UnkMotion7_Coll`, `itWhitebea_UnkMotion1_Anim`, `it_802E3DA0`, `it_802E4464`, and `it_2725_Logic9_Dropped`.

Added and adjusted item data layouts in `itCommonItems.h`: extended `itLugiaAttributes`, changed `itDosei_ItemVars.xDE0` from `s32` to `f32`, added `itTincleAttributes`, added `itKabigon_ItemVars`, and extended `itWhiteBea_ItemVars`/`itWhiteBeaAttributes`.
Added `itKabigon_ItemVars kabigon` to the `Item` item-var union in `src/melee/it/types.h`, and updated Kabigon code to use `ip->xDD4_itemVar.kabigon` instead of generic Pokemon fields.

Replaced several `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` header declarations with typed prototypes, including `it_802CA074(Item_GObj*)`, `it_802D1A44(Item_GObj*)`, `it_802E0D9C(Item_GObj*)`, and `it_802B43D0(Item_GObj*, HSD_GObj*)`; `itlugia.h` also exposes `extern f32 it_804DD450`.

Touched Ness PK Flash and PK Thunder Trail code mostly for include/prototype/float-literal cleanup in the visible diff; the title/body list these files as part of the batch, but the strongest placeholder-to-C evidence is in the other item files.

decomp.dev reported 14 new 100% matches and 7 improvements in unmatched items; the PR body claims all functions are 100% matches in objdiff, so there is some evidence mismatch between bot summary and PR text.
smart_moves: Resolved reviewer concern by using a species-specific `itKabigon_ItemVars` layout rather than type-punning several Pokemon-specific fields through anonymous unions in the generic `itPokemon_ItemVars`.

Kept item behavior expressed through existing engine helpers such as item state changes, collision helpers, JObj animation calls, `efSync_Spawn`, `Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset`, and mp collision facing updates instead of open-coding subsystem behavior.
Updated headers and includes alongside implementations, which turns formerly opaque calls into typed interfaces and reduces future dependence on placeholder prototypes.
Preserved match-sensitive control flow and stack/layout artifacts where needed, including `PAD_STACK(8)`, nested null checks, temporary variables, explicit casts, and literal suffix choices.
Annotated new struct fields with item-var offsets such as `ip+DD4`, `ip+E34`, and `ip+E48`, making the new layouts easier to audit against item memory.
decomp_lessons: Pokemon item variables are not safely interchangeable: when a species needs different field types or semantics, create a dedicated ItemVars type and union member rather than widening a shared Pokemon struct with ambiguous unions.
Special-attributes discoveries should be captured in named item attribute structs even when field names remain unknown; offset comments are valuable until semantics are known.
When decompiling item callbacks, update the corresponding `.h` prototypes from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` at the same time so later PRs can reuse correct signatures.

For item matching, small expression-shape details can matter: local variable lifetimes, scoped temporaries, exact literal forms, and apparently redundant branches may all be intentional matching tactics.
Bot match reports and PR summaries can diverge; future reviewers should verify final diff and objdiff/decomp.dev status before relying on the claimed function count.
naming_conventions: Species/item variable structs follow `it<ItemName>_ItemVars`, e.g. `itKabigon_ItemVars`, with a lower-case union member in `Item`, e.g. `kabigon`.
Attribute structs follow `it<ItemName>Attributes`, e.g. `itTincleAttributes`, `itWhiteBeaAttributes`, and local `itKabigonAttributes`.
Unknown fields remain offset-style names such as `x44`, `x48`, `xE44`, and `xE48`, often paired with offset comments rather than speculative semantic names.
Unknown functions retain address-style names like `it_802E3DA0`, while known callbacks use item/motion suffixes such as `itTincle_UnkMotion4_Phys` and `itWhitebea_UnkMotion1_Anim`.
Header declarations retain address comments and should prefer typed arguments over `UNK_PARAMS` once call signatures are established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `PAD_STACK(8)` appears in several implemented routines and should be considered a deliberate stack-match aid, not dead code.
The Kabigon effect spawn uses repeated `r = HSD_Randf();` assignments and the expression `2.0F * attr->x18 * r - attr->x18`, preserving a match-friendly arithmetic shape.
Dosei thrown logic keeps a scoped `f32 speed = attr->unk0` before assigning animation frame speed and calling `lb_8000BA0C`, likely to preserve scheduling/register behavior.
WhiteBea facing changes use explicit `int_dir` branches and comparisons like `-1.0f == ip->facing_dir` before `mpCollSetFacingDir`, preserving exact control-flow shape.
Yoyo cleanup preserves nested null checks and iterates `ItemLink` before calling `HSD_GObjPLink_80390228`, then destroys the item with `Item_8026A8EC`.
Some functions require explicit casts such as `(HSD_GObj*) gobj` and callback casts to match existing helper signatures.
review_feedback: ribbanya on `src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h`: "This is wrong; create a new `ItemVars` union for each type of Pokémon." The final diff reflects this by adding `itKabigon_ItemVars` and `ip->xDD4_itemVar.kabigon` usage instead of broadening generic Pokemon vars with anonymous unions.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that add species-specific Pokemon `ItemVars` members after this review feedback.
Search for remaining uses of generic `itPokemon_ItemVars` where a per-Pokemon struct would be more accurate.
Audit whether the bot-reported partial matches for `it_3F14_Logic7_Thrown`, `itWhitebea_UnkMotion1_Anim`, `itLugia_UnkMotion4_Phys`, and `itOldottosea_UnkMotion7_Coll` were later made 100%.
Search item decomp PRs for `PAD_STACK(8)` and nested null-check patterns to reuse matching tactics.
Compare PR title/body count of 24 functions with final diff placeholder replacements and decomp.dev report before using the count as authoritative.
